canonical_artifacts.name: manifest
canonical_artifacts.why_it_matters: Discovers all examples, commands, schemas, docs, and success signals from one JSON file.

canonical_artifacts.name: checksums
canonical_artifacts.why_it_matters: Verifies byte-level integrity for canonical checked-in Agent Trust artifacts.

canonical_artifacts.name: doctor
canonical_artifacts.why_it_matters: Runs local consistency checks across fixtures, schemas, manifest, checksums, request wrapper, and verifier.

canonical_artifacts.name: canonical_request
canonical_artifacts.why_it_matters: Shows the accepted versioned input shape.

canonical_artifacts.name: golden_output
canonical_artifacts.why_it_matters: Shows deterministic bundle output for the canonical request.

canonical_artifacts.name: schemas
canonical_artifacts.why_it_matters: Provides request, bundle, and manifest JSON Schemas for mechanical consumers.

canonical_artifacts.name: evidence_transcript
canonical_artifacts.why_it_matters: One command emits the reviewer-facing local evidence transcript, including doctor, adoption, daily-review, and loopback readiness summaries without external action.

canonical_artifacts.name: consumer_decision
canonical_artifacts.why_it_matters: Shows how another local agent can combine manifest, review packet, and doctor output into an integrate/abstain decision.

integration_paths.best_for: Python agents that want the smallest in-process local call.
integration_paths.name: direct_python_import

integration_paths.best_for: Agents and CI jobs that prefer subprocess boundaries and JSON files.
integration_paths.name: local_cli

integration_paths.best_for: Local services that already communicate over HTTP/JSON.
integration_paths.name: local_http_json

integration_paths.best_for: External consumers validating contracts before running integration code.
integration_paths.name: schema_and_golden_output

local_proof_commands.expected_signal: Final doctor JSON reports ok=true and ready_for_local_integration=true.
checks_failed is 0, validation_summary names manifest/checksums/schemas/request/golden output, and review_readiness names local evidence plus what is not proven.

ok=true, artifact is agent_trust_reviewer_evidence_transcript, evidence includes doctor/adoption_readiness/daily_review/loopback_readiness, and next_smallest_safe_step keeps outreach/hosted/wallet/signing/payment/real-money actions stopped.
ok=true and matches_golden=true for the deterministic CLI bundle output.
Contract metadata reports supported contract version and the no-network/no-wallet/no-execution/no-real-money boundary.
decision is integrate_experimentally when manifest, review packet, safety boundary, and doctor checks are coherent.

local_proof_commands.proves: Daily Agent Trust security-and-progress checkpoint passes local release-state, doctor, and Base Sepolia dry-run boundary checks.
Local transcript evidence is coherent enough for human/agent review only, while outreach, live Base Sepolia calls, wallets, signing, payments, and real money remain unauthorized.

local_proof_commands.description: Review sanitized scanner evidence ingestion and deny/review mapping without executing scanners.
Review sanitized agent-security evidence ingestion and task-warrant review/deny mapping without external action.

local_proof_commands.expected_signal: ok=true, status=ready_for_local_review_only, and no outreach/network/wallet/signing/payment/real-money flags are enabled.

decision is loopback_ready_for_controlled_edge_prep; the dedicated Agent Trust MVP runs only on 127.0.0.1, allowlisted discovery/contract/bundle paths return 200, forbidden control-console paths return 404, and no hosted availability, port opening, wallet, signing, payment, real-money, or outreach action occurs.

ok=true, response_packet_valid=true, reviewer_response.decision is accepted/rejected/needs_update, and authorized_next_step remains local-only while posting/outreach/account/wallet/signing/network/payment/real-money actions stay unauthorized.
ok=true, decision=first_pilot_review_path_ready, provenance classification is provenance_evidence_not_trust, and tool/MCP/API/wallet/signing/payment/outreach remain unauthorized.

name: Agent Trust external review packet
purpose: Machine-readable review surface for agents or developers evaluating whether the local Agent Trust examples packet is safe, coherent, and worth integrating experimentally.
review_questions: Can your agent find the request, output, schema, verifier, doctor, and safety boundary without reading source code?
Can your agent run the evidence transcript as the single reviewer-facing local proof packet before deciding whether to inspect the lower-level doctor, adoption, daily-review, or loopback outputs?
Does the bundle contain enough evidence fields for your agent to decide whether to pay for or use a tool/API before acting?
Which integration path would you choose first: direct import, CLI, local HTTP, or schema/golden-output validation?
What missing field would make this trustworthy enough for a real pre-action gate later?
Does intended_integration_context match a real pre-action gate in your agent loop, framework, or tool runner?
Does the review decision gate draw the right boundary between local review readiness and actions that still require separate authorization?
Can your agent provide a sanitized scanner finding summary, without raw payloads or secrets, that Agent Trust can map to proceed/review/deny before a risky action?
Can your agent turn the integration ticket into a concrete first adapter or field request without needing hosted service behavior, credentials, wallet access, or outreach?
Can your agent provide a sanitized memory/planning/tool/MCP/payment/contact risk summary with task-warrant fields that Agent Trust can map to proceed/review/deny before action?
Can your agent run the loopback readiness proof and correctly keep it framed as local-only evidence rather than hosted availability?

Can your agent or reviewer return a structured accepted/rejected/needs_update response packet after inspecting the local proof, so the next Agent Trust product move is based on actionable signal rather than vague interest?
Can a framework/tooling reviewer use the at-fw-001 reviewer packet to return integrate, abstain, or revise without external action or secret/wallet/payment behavior?
Can your agent run the first-pilot review check and verify review-only readiness plus provenance_evidence_not_trust without manually diffing multiple outputs?

safety_boundary.notes: Review commands are local checkout commands. They inspect files, run local Python contract entrypoints, and never contact external services, wallets, facilitators, or tool endpoints.

stop_conditions: Do not publish or announce externally without explicit human confirmation.
Do not connect wallets, private keys, facilitators, paid endpoints, or real-money flows from this packet.
Do not treat a local mock approval as permission to spend money.
Stop and ask for review if a reviewer requests hosted service behavior, account setup, credential handling, or legal/compliance commitments.
success_signals: Reviewer can run the evidence transcript as one local proof packet and see doctor, adoption, daily-review, and loopback readiness summarized without hosted availability, wallet, signing, payment, real-money, or outreach action.
Reviewer can run one local command and report ok=true without reading implementation source.
Reviewer can name the first integration path they would try and one missing evidence field.
Another agent can use manifest + review packet + schemas to decide whether the local contract is worth experimental integration.
Another agent can run the consumer decision example and get integrate_experimentally without reading implementation source.
Doctor output explicitly separates local proof from non-proven hosted, wallet, real-money, legal/compliance, and demand signals.
Reviewer can point to intended_integration_context as the place where Agent Trust would sit before a risky tool/API/payment-like action.
Reviewer can run the review decision gate and see ready_for_human_or_agent_review_only without any outreach, wallet, signing, live testnet, payment, or real-money action.
Reviewer can run the integration ticket and name the smallest acceptable first adapter or missing evidence field while keeping all external actions unauthorized.
Reviewer can run the agent-security evidence packet and see memory/context, authorization-control, orchestration, and task-warrant risks mapped to review/deny without external action.
Reviewer can run the loopback readiness proof and distinguish local 127.0.0.1:8766 readiness from any non-proven hosted/public availability claim.

Reviewer can run the reviewer response packet and return an accepted/rejected/needs_update signal with explicit evidence fields, rubric results, next-product-move recommendation, and hard stops before outreach, posting, accounts, wallets, signing, network calls, payments, or real money.

Reviewer can inspect the at-fw-001 reviewer packet and return a deterministic integrate/abstain/revise signal while all outreach, posting, network exposure, wallet, signing, payment, real-money, secrets, and repository-settings actions remain stopped.
Reviewer can run the first-pilot review check and get a single first_pilot_review_path_ready verdict while all tool/MCP/API/wallet/signing/payment/outreach actions remain unauthorized.
